name: Eddie Fisher
abstract: Edwin Jack "Eddie" Fisher (August 10, 1928 – September 22, 2010) was an American singer and actor. Popular during the 1950s, he sold millions of records and hosted his own TV show, The Eddie Fisher Show.

concerts.label: Pop Concerts in New York: Heart Fund Program

works.t: Der schwarze Hecht: O mein Papa

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Mead
about_quotes.evidence: Eddie Fisher is mentioned by the interviewer as a singer of a mid-fifties hit, with no personal interaction or connection described with the interviewee.
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Mead interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, June 1, 2006

about_quotes.speaker: Onaje Allan Gumbs
about_quotes.evidence: I remember when I was three years old I would walk around the room singing “Oh My Papa,” by Eddie Fisher.
about_quotes.doc_title: Onaje Allan Gumbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Vince Giordano
about_quotes.evidence: He got an occasional call from Manhattan Transfer to write a few charts for them and he called me up and said, “I’ve got this young group they want me to recreate some Goldkette things. about_quotes.doc_title: Vince Giordano interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 11, 2007
